events.reason: Independently verified the Story's stated intent (reduce the largest workspace-infra->story violation pair) matches exactly what happened, down to the AC-1 edge-count reconciliation. Intent and outcome match with no inferential leap needed.

Verified the workflow current-reality claim directly: git diff shows managed-worktree.js, managed-worktree-gate.js, and decision-records.js are byte-identical across the change (only target-model.json's classification of decision-records.js moved). Reran the cited e2e suite (12/12 pass) rather than trusting the recorded artifact.

Verified IDC-INV-001 directly in src/performance-evidence.js: the hasUserPerceivedEvidence/readinessKind branches are untouched; only the normalizeActiveStories import line changed source module. The final squashed commit's src/docs content carries the structured inherited_behaviors entries in the spec.json artifact the Requirement Gate reads.

The cited decision_record is topically about a different, tangential boundary question (.vibepro/ commit policy), not the actual module-reclassification boundary this Story makes -- a citation mismatch. However the architecture_doc evidence (Story reason: frontmatter's boundary section, plus Spec IDC-CONTRACT-001/003's explicit responsibility rationale) substantively addresses the real boundary question and was independently verified against the actual code (decision-records.js's imports/consumers).

The cited failure-mode evidence (targeted unit suite + two e2e replay files) is the correct kind of evidence for workflow-shaped changes, reran both with passing results, directly exercising the managed-worktree/decision-record and story-discovery paths this refactor touches.
Same evidence set as failure_modes/current_reality, independently rerun with passing results (88/88 unit, 12/12 e2e), directly covering the changed files and their real call paths.

The matched evidence list is generic boilerplate reused across all four axis items, so verified independently: git diff shows src/cli.js is completely untouched, and a repo-wide grep confirms normalizeActiveStories/isArchived have no remaining import from ./story-manager.js anywhere. No CLI flag, output format, or config schema changed.

Verified directly: decision-records.js (the only file in this diff that touches persistent state) has a completely empty diff. The rest is pure function relocation and JSON module-classification metadata. No migration, cache, query, or idempotency-sensitive code is touched.

This Story makes no performance claim and touches no runtime/compiler-semantic code path (no algorithmic change, no async/sync change) -- it is a pure static dependency-graph classification and function-relocation refactor. The axis is vacuously satisfied.

Changed-file set is small and single-intent: 5 src files (3 one-line import retargets, 1 pure function relocation, 1 file receiving it) plus registration scaffolding. Read start-to-finish, this is a coherent, single-purpose PR a reviewer could assess in one sitting.
Classifier marked this not_required. Verified correct: this diff touches no parser/JSON-extraction code, so exclusion is appropriate rather than a dodge.

decision-records.js's own logic is diff-empty, and decision-records.test.js was rerun as part of the 88/88 passing targeted suite, directly exercising the lifecycle this failure mode concerns. The only change affecting this area is a target-model.json module reclassification, re-passed by architecture-conformance.test.js.

Reran the two cited e2e files: 12/12 pass, directly replaying the workflow/state-transition paths (gate ac1-ac7, execution-dag runtime smoke) that touch managed-worktree.js/managed-worktree-gate.js, both byte-identical pre/post change.
